title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
authors: Patrick Lewis, Ethan Perez, Aleksandra Piktus, Fabio Petroni et al.
hook: Imagine a chatbot that can instantly look up facts before answering you.
promise: You will understand how RAG lets language models retrieve knowledge before generating answers.
prerequisite_note: Bring a curiosity for how language models might pull in outside information while they write.

glossary.plain: The knowledge stored inside a model's parameters after training.
External knowledge sources, like a database or documents, that the model can consult at runtime.
A system that finds relevant documents from a large collection based on a query.
A neural network that turns one sequence, like a question, into another, like an answer.
A way to represent documents as vectors so the model can quickly search for similar ones.
A problem that requires specific factual information to answer correctly.

cards.title: Let models look things up
body: Learn how RAG lets AI models search for facts before writing answers.

cards.title: Why memory matters for language models
body: Imagine asking a language model for a recent fact, like the latest Nobel Prize winner. If it was trained before that event, it cannot know the answer.
What if the model could instantly search Wikipedia or another resource for up-to-date facts? Retrieval-Augmented Generation, or RAG, makes this possible.

cards.title: How RAG connects searching and writing
body: Traditional models answer based only on what they remember from training, missing new or rare facts.
RAG first uses a retriever to find relevant documents, then feeds them into a language model that writes the answer using both its memory and the new information.
The result is a model that can answer questions with up-to-date details, and even cite where it found them.
cards.caption: RAG combines searching and generation for more factual, flexible answers.

cards.title: How answers change when models can retrieve
body: Imagine asking for the population of a small city. Toggle to see what happens.
cards.offNote: Model guesses based on outdated or missing information from its training.
cards.onNote: Model retrieves the latest data from Wikipedia, then writes a more accurate answer.

cards.title: Blending retrieval with generation
body: RAG’s key trick is to let the language model consult an external memory, like a search engine, while generating each answer.
Instead of memorizing everything, the model learns to retrieve relevant passages and combine them with its own knowledge to write more factual, grounded responses.

cards.title: How much should the model rely on retrieval?
body: Slide to see what happens as the model trusts retrieved documents more or less.
cards.low: Relies only on training memory, may miss rare or new facts.
cards.mid: Balances both memories, using retrieval for specifics and model memory for language.
cards.high: Copies mostly from retrieved documents, but may lose fluency or context.

cards.title: Choose RAG’s key ingredients
body: Pick the components that let RAG models combine searching and generating.

choices.label: Dense vector retriever
choices.note: Finds relevant passages for each question.

choices.label: Wikipedia index
choices.note: Provides the external knowledge base to search.

choices.label: Token-level conditioning
choices.note: Lets the model switch passages as it writes each word.

choices.label: End-to-end fine-tuning
choices.note: Trains both retrieval and generation together.

choices.label: Non-parametric memory
choices.note: Stores up-to-date facts outside the model parameters.

cards.title: RAG sets new benchmarks in open-domain QA
body: RAG models outperformed previous methods on open-domain question answering, especially where up-to-date or rare facts were needed.
They also generated answers that were more specific, diverse, and grounded in real sources, beating parametric-only models.

bars.label: Open-domain QA accuracy
bars.note: RAG achieves a new benchmark high

cards.caption: RAG’s results showed clear improvements in factual and diverse answer generation.

cards.title: Why RAG changed language models
body: RAG was a turning point because it let models answer questions using current, external knowledge, not just what they memorized during training.
It paved the way for future systems that blend search and generation, making AI more trustworthy and adaptable.

recap.label: Ask
recap.note: A question arrives for the model.

recap.label: Retrieve
recap.note: The model searches documents for relevant facts.

recap.label: Generate
recap.note: It writes an answer using both memories.

recap.label: Ground
recap.note: The answer cites and relies on real sources.

cards.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
body: Read the full paper to see how RAG changed the way language models use knowledge.
